evidence: QueryExecutor interface has 6 methods with a single concrete implementation (BlackLabQueryExecutor). The interface Javadoc explicitly acknowledges this: 'The single production implementation means the interface acts primarily as a testability seam rather than an abstraction boundary.' This is an honest single-impl interface — acceptable but worth monitoring.
CollocateQueryHelper is a package-private class used exclusively by BlackLabQueryExecutor. It is not an interface, just a helper object — appropriate decomposition without unnecessary abstraction.

ExploreResponseAssembler is a final class of static methods with no state — a pure static utility. Its methods are coherent (all transform exploration/comparison results to JSON maps), so this is a focused utility rather than a dumping ground.
SemanticFieldExplorer acts as a facade over MultiSeedExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator. It adds policy (validation, guard clauses) and composes the sub-objects — this pays for itself.

RelationConfig is a record with substantial embedded logic: buildFullPattern, buildCollocateReversePattern, computeCollocatePosGroup, extractLabelContent, posGroupFromConstraint, posGroupForPrefix, computeDeprel — 8 methods plus 2 private static helpers. This gives the record a wider responsibility surface than pure data carrying.

GrammarConfigLoader is a final class with a private constructor and only static methods — a pure loader utility. Its methods (load, fromReader, parse, readConfigFile, parseRelations, parseRelation, validatePositions, etc.) are all tightly focused on loading; no scope creep.

HttpApiUtils contains sendJsonResponse, sendError, sendOptionsResponse, sendBinaryResponse, requireMethod, parseQueryParams, requireParam, parseIntParam, wrapWithErrorHandling — 9 methods spanning HTTP response writing, request parsing, and error wrapping. This is a mild dumping ground: response sending and parameter parsing are distinct concerns.
CqlUtils has 5 methods (extractConstraintAttribute, escapeForRegex, splitCqlTokens, substituteAtPosition, mergeLemmaConstraint) — all are tightly related CQL text manipulation; a coherent utility.

Main.java handleBlackLabIndexCommand, handleBlackLabQueryCommand, handleBlackLabServerCommand each inline argument parsing, which means CLI parsing logic is not abstracted. However, given there are only 3 commands, this is not yet a problem.

ExplorationHandlers (not fully shown but partially inspected) passes around raw Map<String, String> query parameter maps throughout handlers rather than typed parameter objects — callers must know the key strings.

strengths: CollocateQueryHelper is a well-justified extraction: it encapsulates a coherent algorithm (collocate search, frequency ranking, hit collection) that was too large to live in BlackLabQueryExecutor directly.
ExploreResponseAssembler is a focused, static utility with strong cohesion — all methods transform the same family of model objects.
SemanticFieldExplorer facade adds real policy (validation, guard clauses, fallback logic) rather than being a thin pass-through.
CqlUtils methods are tightly related and form a minimal coherent set.
abstraction_fitness.issue_character: The issues are moderate: a single-impl interface documented as intentional, a record with growing method responsibility, and HttpApiUtils as a mild utility dumping ground — individually minor but collectively reducing the signal-to-noise of the abstraction layer.
abstraction_fitness.score_rationale: The codebase scores in the upper sixties: most abstractions earn their place and the key utilities are coherent. The single-impl QueryExecutor interface is acknowledged in its own Javadoc, RelationConfig's embedded method count has grown past pure-data-carrier territory, and HttpApiUtils blends HTTP response writing with request parsing. These are drag, not crisis — typical of a mid-maturity codebase where some abstractions were introduced for real need but have drifted slightly.

issues.summary: RelationConfig record has grown beyond pure data carrier into a logic-heavy class with 8+ methods including pattern building, POS group computation, and deprel extraction
evidence: RelationConfig (a record) defines: buildFullPattern(String), buildFullPattern(String, String), buildCollocateReversePattern(), computeCollocatePosGroup(String), extractLabelContent(String, int), posGroupFromConstraint(String), posGroupForPrefix(String, String), computeDeprel() — 8 methods plus the toJson() serialization method.
computeCollocatePosGroup and posGroupForPrefix contain pattern-matching logic over CQL strings that would be better in CqlUtils or a dedicated PatternAnalyzer.
buildCollocateReversePattern uses a switch on PosGroup — domain policy that belongs in a config utility, not in the data record itself.
issues.suggestion: Extract CQL-analysis methods (computeCollocatePosGroup, extractLabelContent, posGroupFromConstraint, posGroupForPrefix) into CqlUtils or a new RelationPatternAnalyzer class. Keep buildFullPattern and buildCollocateReversePattern in RelationConfig as they express the record's primary transformation contract. computeDeprel can remain as it reads record fields directly.

issues.summary: HttpApiUtils mixes HTTP response writing with request parameter parsing — two distinct concerns in one utility class
evidence: HttpApiUtils has response methods (sendJsonResponse, sendError, sendOptionsResponse, sendBinaryResponse, requireMethod, sendOptionsResponse) and request methods (parseQueryParams, requireParam, parseIntParam) in one class.
A developer looking for 'how to parse query parameters' and a developer looking for 'how to send a JSON error' both land in HttpApiUtils — the utility serves two distinct caller needs.
issues.suggestion: Split into HttpResponseUtils (response writing, CORS, method enforcement) and HttpRequestUtils (query parameter parsing, typed parameter extraction). Both remain package-private. This makes each utility easier to test in isolation and reduces the surface a caller must scan.

root_causes: RelationConfig method sprawl is a gradual accumulation: each method was added to make RelationConfig self-contained, but the record has now exceeded pure-data-carrier territory.
HttpApiUtils grew organically as the API layer was built; response and request utilities were added to the same class because they are both 'HTTP utilities.'
possible_false_positives: QueryExecutor single-impl: explicitly acknowledged in Javadoc as intentional testability seam — not a genuine abstraction fitness problem.
